hero_summary: Zanjaran is a relatively uncommon maqam, and one of the maqamat that does not settle on a single completely fixed definition across all schools. In the nearest practical Arabic formulation, it is understood as a hybrid maqam combining a Hijaz root with an Ajam expansion in the upper region.
short_identity: A hybrid between Hijaz and Ajam/Jaharkah: a Hijaz root with Ajam on the fourth or ghammaz.

quick_facts.karar: It varies according to transposition and school, and in this round there is no single Arabic transposition documented strongly enough to impose as final
quick_facts.ghammaz: The fourth or ghammaz, as the place where the distinguishing Ajam appears
quick_facts.upper_jins: Jins Ajam on the fourth or ghammaz
quick_facts.common_start: It often begins by stabilizing the Hijaz color at the root, then opens upward into Ajam
quick_facts.character: Hybrid, tense at the root, and brighter above because of the Ajam space

sayr.summary: The nearest practical sayr of Zanjaran, according to this formulation, begins by stabilizing the Hijaz color at the root. It then does not keep the upper region purely Hijaz-based or Nahawand-based as in some nearby maqamat, but instead opens into Ajam on the fourth or ghammaz. That is where the real difference of the maqam emerges. After that, the jawab may expand further, and then the line returns at the end to the tonic. The essential route is therefore: a Hijaz root, then an Ajam upper space, then a return to the tonic.
resting_tones: The tonic degree from which the Hijaz jins begins
The fourth or ghammaz: because it is the place where the Ajam appears and distinguishes Zanjaran from simple Hijaz
motion_notes: Zanjaran does not settle on one absolutely fixed definition across all schools
Some traditions place it in the Hijaz family, while others lean toward the Ajam family
What remains firm in this formulation is the tension between a Hijaz root and an Ajam expansion
The safest conclusion here is to preserve the relation between Hijaz below and Ajam on the fourth, rather than impose a single transposition that is not universally agreed upon
common_path: Show the Ajam on the fourth or ghammaz clearly
Expand in the jawab when needed
common_modulations: Ajam on the fourth or ghammaz as the maqam’s distinguishing sign
If the upper region remains purely Hijaz-based, the ear moves toward Hijaz rather than Zanjaran
If the upper region shifts into Nahawand, the sound moves away from the practical formulation adopted here
Its family classification may shift according to the school, but the nearest practical Arabic reading is the meeting of Hijaz with Ajam/Jaharkah

examples.title: Asir al-'Ishq
examples.note: An important historical witness proving that Zanjaran entered real modern Arabic composition

examples.title: Ya Halawat al-Dunya
examples.note: A useful archived press witness to the maqam’s presence, though less solid than the Dawud Husni example

reference_recordings.title: Asir al-'Ishq
reference_recordings.note: The clearest witness in this round to Zanjaran’s presence in actual Arabic composition

reference_recordings.note: A supporting rather than primary reference, because it comes from a press notice rather than a full modal analysis
